intro: Notodanthonia vestita, also known as Tasmanian blue grass or Blue Button-grass, is a perennial grass species native to Tasmania. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Notodanthonia vestita include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to soothe skin irritations.
